data.title: Pfizer gets ACIP backing as it votes to expand RSV vaccine recommendation
data.description:
The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention’s vaccine panel voted to expand its recommendation for the use of respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) shots in adults with 50-59 years of age at increased risk of RSV-associated lower respiratory tract disease, Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) said on Wednesday.

This decision includes Abrysvo, approved for the prevention of RSV-related respiratory diseases in adults aged 18 to 59 at increased risk of severe disease.

The CDC's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) had earlier said it will weigh lower age for the vaccine, having current recommendation for adults aged 75 years and older and at-risk adults aged 60 to 74 years.

"The updated ACIP recommendation, which lowers the recommended age for RSV vaccination from 60 to 50 for high-risk adults, is pending final approval by the director of the CDC and the Department of Health and Human Services," Pfizer said in a statement.

data.title: Bank OZK Q1 earnings top consensus as noninterest income gains; NII guide reiterated
data.description:
Bank OZK (NASDAQ:OZK) on Wednesday turned in Q1 earnings that surpassed the average analyst estimate as a sequential rise in noninterest income and lending activity both helped to offset a decline in net interest income.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.47, topping the $1.44 consensus, fell from $1.56 in the earlier quarter and $1.51 in the year-ago period.

Net interest income edged down to 1.3% sequentially and 0.6% Y/Y to $374.5M. Net interest margin of 4.31% vs. 4.33% in Q4 and 4.71% in Q1 2024.

The lender still expects further growth in average earnings assets and record NII in one or two quarters in 2025 and for the full year. 

OZK shares inched up 0.4% in after-hours trading.

Provision for credit losses totaled $38.4M for Q1, up from $37.2M in Q4 and down from $42.9M in Q1 2024.

Pretax pre-provision net revenue was $262.3M, down from $272.2M in Q4 and $272.7M a year before.

Noninterest income accelerated 5.4% Q/Q and 19.4% Y/Y to $34.7M.

Period-end deposits climbed to $31.9B in Q1 from $31.0B in the previous quarter. Period-end loans rose to $31.1B from $30.0B in Q4.

Conference call on April 17 at 8:30 a.m. ET.

data.title: Live Oak Acquisition announces separate trading of its class A ordinary shares and warrants
data.description:
• Live Oak Acquisition (NASDAQ:LOKVU) said on Wednesday that, commencing April 21, 2025, holders of the units sold in the company’s initial public offering may elect to separately trade the company’s class A ordinary shares and warrants included in the units. 
  • No fractional warrants will be issued upon separation of the units and only whole warrants will trade. 
  • The class A ordinary shares and warrants that are separated will trade on the Nasdaq Global Market under the symbols “LOKV” and “LOKVW,” respectively. Those units not separated will continue to trade on the Nasdaq Global Market under the symbol “LOKVU.”

data.title: British American Tobacco snaps six straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Shares of British American Tobacco (NYSE:BTI) snapped six straight sessions of gains, as the stock closed 1.16% down at $41.83 on Wednesday.

The company gained 7% in the last six trading sessions. The stock closed 0.74% higher at $42.32 on Tuesday.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating system has rated British American Tobacco stock as a Hold with a score of 3.35 out of 5, with an A+ on the prospect of valuation, while a D in revisions dragged down the rating.

Wall Street analysts are also cautious and rated the stock as a Hold.

Seeking Alpha analyst Manika Premsingh noted that the company also continued to pivot towards tobacco free products, which provide some assurance of its future at a time when tobacco products are losing popularity. 

This is evident from the fact that the company anticipates a 2% decline in the global tobacco industry's volumes in 2025, Premsingh said, adding that “By contrast, consumers of smokeless products, including traditional oral ones, are growing as reflected in a 14% increase in the same for BAT in 2024. This is also seen in the solid growth in the new categories segment during the year.”

Seeking Alpha analysts are bullish about the company and rated it as a Buy.

BTI gained over 16.50% so far this year, outperforming the broader S&P 500 Index, which declined more than 9%.
